english: give as a present; send to

etymology.description: 送(送) (sòng, "send; deliver; see off") refers to the act of transferring or conveying something to another person. 給(給) (gěi, "give; for; to") involves bestowing or providing something to someone else.
etymology.interpretation: 送(送) (sòng, "send; deliver; see off") emphasizes the action of conveying or dispatching an item or person to a particular destination or recipient, which aligns with giving something. 給(給) (gěi, "give; for; to") adds the sense of transferring possession or making a gift to another person. Together, these components naturally combine to convey the idea of sending or giving something as a present.

examples.chinese: 他送给我一本书(他送給我一本書)。
examples.pinyin: Tā sònggěi wǒ yī běn shū.
examples.english: He gave me a book as a present.

examples.chinese: 我想送给她一朵玫瑰花(我想送給她一朵玫瑰花)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng sònggěi tā yī duǒ méiguī huā.
examples.english: I want to give her a rose.

examples.chinese: 过生日时(過生日時)，她送给了我一个惊喜(她送給了我一個驚喜)。
examples.pinyin: Guò shēngrì shí, tā sònggěi le wǒ yī gè jīngxǐ.
examples.english: On my birthday, she gave me a surprise.
